headline: Pangkor Island Waste Generation Exceeds Disposal Capacity by 40%
context: Manjung Municipal Council data shows Pangkor Island generates 42 tonnes of waste daily against a disposal capacity of 30 tonnes, with excess waste stockpiled on the island.

cards.big: Pangkor Island generates 40% more waste than it can dispose of.
cards.sub: 12 tonnes per day are stockpiled. The island has no incinerator, compactor, or barge-based transfer.

cards.big: Tourism accounts for 60% of Pangkor's waste during peak season.
cards.sub: Hotel and resort operators pay standard waste fees. No tourism-linked waste surcharge exists.

cards.big: 3 illegal dump sites on Pangkor are within 200m of the coast.
cards.sub: Leachate testing shows E. coli levels 14x safe limits entering the marine environment.

cards.big: Tioman Island implemented a waste barge system in 2022 at RM1.8M.
cards.sub: Pangkor has not adopted the model despite comparable geography and waste volumes.

cards.big: Marketing an island as paradise while stockpiling waste on it is not tourism. It is a countdown to collapse.
Island waste management requires dedicated solutions — barges, zero-waste policies, tourist levies. Mainland approaches do not work when there is nowhere to put the waste.
